content: PR #12 now contains 5 commits: native DB resolution, locate command, PR review fixes + tests, stealth/unstealth commands + guide, and doc cross-linking. Rebased cleanly on main after PR #13 (test coverage audit with origin tracking) merged

Add Related guides footer to all docs/guides/*.md files for cross-discoverability. Trim README worktree section from 10 lines to 3 with deep link. Trim BDC_GUIDE stealth commands from 3 lines to 1 with guide link
User wants mode switching to be a one-liner in both directions. Also wants docs trimmed — detailed guides should live in their own files, README and BDC_GUIDE should link out rather than duplicate

Every bdc command that calls getStore() must also defer closeStore() or writes may not flush. This pattern was already followed by all existing commands but was missing from the new stealth commands initially

SQLite config writes were not persisting across process invocations. Root cause: exportForUnstealth() spawns bdc export as a subprocess that opens its own DB connection, creating lock contention with the parent's SetConfig write. Fix: write config and closeStore() before any subprocess invocations

Implement bdc stealth and bdc unstealth as separate top-level commands rather than flags on init. Both are idempotent, preserve all data, and handle edge cases (empty gitignore cleanup, hook section removal from shared hooks, git rm --cached for tracked files)

No stealth-to-normal or normal-to-stealth transition exists in the codebase. stealth_mode config is written at init but never read at runtime. No hook removal, no gitignore cleanup, no exclude file cleanup
Create docs/guides/stealth-mode.md as the canonical reference for stealth workflow, worktree topology, AI agent awareness chain, and mode switching

Stealth mode works end-to-end for worktree agents: global hooks fire bdc prime, global CLAUDE.md teaches the protocol, git-common-dir resolves the DB. Three independent mechanisms, all machine-global or git-tracked — no per-worktree setup needed
Stealth mode may not work transparently for AI agents in worktrees — need to trace the full chain from SessionStart hook through DB resolution to confirm

Implemented origin tracking (v0.5.0): --origin flag on capture, bdc origin set/show/clear, bdc origins discovery, source_ref index migration, --origin filter on all query commands, origin display in show, updated all docs (lifecycle, project-config, README, BDC_GUIDE, prime). AI prefix auto-detection for claude/cursor/codex/warp/gemini/zed/opencode.

AI tool origin prefixes: claude, cursor, codex, warp, gemini, zed, opencode → ai-session. Removed conductor (it runs on claude/codex sessions). slack/git types preserved for import codepaths but --origin auto-detection only distinguishes AI tools vs everything else.

Keep ai-session as source_type name (not ai-agent). It's already stored in existing DB records, semantically correct (the origin IS a session), and renaming requires a migration for no user benefit. The origin prefix string already identifies the specific tool.

Brian: (1) bump version to 0.5.0, (2) update .md docs with origin setup, (3) add warp/gemini/zed/opencode to ai-session prefixes, remove conductor, (4) reconsider whether slack/git need separate source_types or if only AI tools should be grouped under ai-session

Don't modify external_ref_mappings at all. Session-to-thread relationship derived from insights: SELECT DISTINCT thread_id FROM insights WHERE source_ref = ?. No new tables, no role column, just one index migration.
CLI flag named --origin (not --source-ref or --external-id). Distinct from all existing terms (source, thread, type, author). Maps internally to InsightSource.Ref / source_ref column.

external_ref_mappings has external_ref TEXT PRIMARY KEY — one external ref maps to exactly one thread. A session touching multiple threads violates this constraint. Session tracking must go on insights (source_ref), not external_ref_mappings.

source_ref column already exists in DB schema (insights table) but capture CLI never populates it. Only import codepaths set it. This is our foundation — no new columns needed, just surface the existing field.
Merged as PR #8, version bumped to 0.4.1

Added Prerequisites section to BDC_GUIDE.md with inline install/init instructions. Agents ask user for team mode vs stealth mode before running bdc init. Enhanced bdc prime to emit stderr hint when repo tracks .beadcrumbs/ but isn't initialized locally. Added guard clause to CLAUDE.md.
No existing skill or auto-setup mechanism exists for bdc — agents hit 'command not found' or 'database not found' errors when bdc is missing

Added generic bdc thread link command accepting any external ref format, and --bead flag on thread new mirroring --linear. Migration 006 retroactively fixes orphaned bead thread IDs. Enhanced bdc trace to find insights via both dependency chains and thread-to-bead mappings. Shipped as v0.4.0 in PR #7.
Rename 'dual linking' to 'multi-system linking' — the feature supports arbitrary external systems, not just two.

Keep thread-level linking and dependency-level linking (--spawns) as separate mechanisms. Thread-level = grouping/context (which tracker is this thread about), dependency-level = causation (this insight led to that task). Both remain explicit, no auto-creation between them.

Normalize bead IDs through the external ref pipeline: bd-abc1 → bead:abc1 canonical format, routed through resolveExternalThreadRef() to create real threads with external_ref_mappings, same as linear:ENG-456 already does.

The external_ref_mappings table already supports one-to-many relationships (one thread → many external refs). Multi-system linking was architecturally possible — the problem was only that bead IDs bypassed this pipeline entirely.

Bead IDs (bd-xxx) bypass the thread system entirely — in capture.go:250-252, --thread bd-abc1 sets insight.ThreadID directly without creating a real thread or external ref mapping. This makes dual-linking impossible and causes a latent FK violation since PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON and insights.thread_id references threads(id).

Implementation complete: CSV/JSONL import with auto-detecting column mapping, Slack API client (stdlib only, rate limiting, pagination), OAuth v2 flow (local server + manual fallback), user name cache, message-to-insight converter, full CLI command set (bdc slack auth/status/channels/fetch/config), 35 tests all passing, integration guide doc. PR #6 created.

Plan approved. Implementation order: Batch 1 (foundation: timestamp utils, export functions, slack ref pattern) -> Batch 2 (generic import: CSV/JSONL parsers + CLI) in parallel with Batch 3 (Slack library: API client, user cache, OAuth, converter) -> Batch 4 (Slack CLI commands + guide doc). Created Linear issues AVO-5 through AVO-14 for tracking.

User preferences: 1) OAuth should try local server first, fall back to manual code paste. 2) No confirmation prompt on import -- just preview + save like current behavior. 3) Create fine-grained Linear issues per work item (~8-10 issues).

Two-phase approach: Phase 1 builds generic CSV/JSONL import with column mapping (works for any data source). Phase 2 builds Slack API integration with OAuth v2 flow using local HTTP server for the auth dance. Slack client uses Go stdlib only (net/http), no external deps. Multi-workspace handled naturally by per-project .beadcrumbs/beadcrumbs.db.

Existing codebase already has Slack export file parser (internal/import/slack.go) with noise filtering and type detection. Linear integration uses adapter pattern wrapping CLI tools. Config stored in SQLite config table with namespace.key pattern. Each project's .beadcrumbs/beadcrumbs.db is gitignored, making per-project token storage natural for multi-workspace support.

Two-phase approach: first build generic import framework (CSV/JSONL from any source), then build Slack-specific integration on top. Slack API requires OAuth v2 which is heavy for a CLI tool - may need a lightweight local server for the auth dance.
Global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md needs manual update: thread ref priority order is wrong (Bead ID first, should be Linear first) and missing bdc linear commands. Cannot edit from sandboxed workspace.

Created dedicated docs/guides/linear.md in OSS repo as the canonical Linear integration guide. Other docs reference it rather than duplicating setup instructions. Balto-pipeline project docs focus on project-specific conventions.
Claude bot flagged unnecessary for/break loop in beadcrumbs-auto-stage.sh — replaced with direct bdc export call

9 docs needed updating across 2 repos: 3 in balto-pipeline (agents/core/beadcrumbs.md, agents/core/linear-beads.md, agents/skills/beadcrumbs-lifecycle.md) and 5 in beadcrumbs OSS (new linear.md guide + updates to README, BDC_GUIDE, lifecycle, project-config). Plus global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md needs manual update.

beadcrumbs PRs #3 and #4 added: bdc linear setup/status/push/link/config, --linear flag on thread new, auto-push on close, 3 CLI adapter support (schpet, finesssee, linearis), env var API key precedence
Existing docs only mention linear:ENG-456 as a thread ref format — need to document the full bdc linear subcommand, auto-push, and --linear flag

Merged as PR #4. Version bumped to 0.3.0. Files changed: cmd/bdc/linear.go (helper + status + push + config help), cmd/bdc/thread.go (linkThreadToLinear + pushLinearSummaryOnClose), cmd/bdc/capture.go (resolveLinearRef).

Implemented Option A (primary) + Option C (enhancement): centralized getLinearConfig() helper replaces 5 duplicated config-read call sites, adds LINEAR_API_KEY and BDC_LINEAR_API_KEY env var overrides. bdc linear status now shows API key source.

Three options considered: (A) per-repo api_key config (already works), (B) wrapper scripts via cli_path (already works), (C) env var override with BDC_LINEAR_API_KEY > LINEAR_API_KEY > config precedence (small code change, adds genuine value for direnv/CI workflows)

Shell aliases (e.g., linear-balto) don't work with Go's exec.Command since aliases are shell-only constructs. The existing cli_path config already supports bare binary names via exec.LookPath, so wrapper scripts work as an alternative.

Config is already per-repo: each bdc init creates an isolated .beadcrumbs/beadcrumbs.db with its own config table. Setting linear.api_key in each project directory is independent. Multi-project already works with zero code changes.
How should bdc support multiple Linear workspaces (e.g., balto and avodaas) with separate API keys? Should it leverage existing shell aliases like linear-balto?
User modified linear.go post-merge to add env var precedence: BDC_LINEAR_API_KEY > LINEAR_API_KEY > config. Also added getLinearConfig() shared helper and bumped to 0.3.0
Implemented 14 files (7 new, 7 modified), 1241 insertions. PR #3 merged. Version bumped 0.1.0 → 0.2.0 → 0.3.0
Auto-push summary on thread close is opt-out (linear.auto_push defaults true). Summary comment groups insights by type: decisions first, then pivots, then discoveries
Graceful degradation — Linear CLI failures never block core bdc operations. Warnings go to stderr. Thread still creates/closes even if Linear fetch/push fails
Separate external_ref_mappings table (not a column on threads) because a thread could link to multiple external systems. Maps linear:ENG-456 to thr-xxxx with cached metadata JSON

Per-repo linear.api_key config stored in .beadcrumbs/beadcrumbs.db config table. runCmd injects LINEAR_API_KEY env var into subprocess. Replaces alias pattern — different repos can auth to different Linear workspaces
Need to support custom aliases like linear-avodaas (LINEAR_API_KEY=xxx linear). Shell aliases aren't visible to Go exec.Command

Adapter pattern — one Adapter interface, three implementations (schpet, finesssee, linearis). Detect() factory auto-finds installed CLIs in preference order. Only internal/linear/ package calls CLI binaries
Support multiple Linear CLI tools — @schpet/linear-cli (primary), finesssee/linear-cli (Rust), and Linearis (Node). Detect what's installed, suggest @schpet as recommended default
Shell out to existing Linear CLI tools rather than importing SDKs — piggyback on existing auth, keep go.mod clean
Want medium integration: bi-directional linking — create insights from Linear issues, attach insight summaries back as Linear comments
External ref parsing exists (linear:ENG-456 format) in internal/beads/integration.go, but resolveThreadRef is a placeholder that stores refs as plain strings with no actual Linear interaction
Does beadcrumbs know how to link/work well with Linear? Can it plug-and-play into existing Linear workflows?

All 7 workstreams complete. Created 5 new files (export.go, 4 docs/guides/), modified 5 files (store.go, init.go, prime.go, BDC_GUIDE.md, README.md, capture.go). Build passes, tests pass, no stale opus-4.5 refs in user-facing code
Generalized all tracker references to show linear:ENG-456, jira:PROJ-123, gh:42 — not just Linear. Open source repo should be tracker-agnostic

7 workstreams: (1) bdc export command, (2) pre-commit hook in init, (3) .gitignore auto-config in init, (4) update BDC_GUIDE.md, (5) update bdc prime output, (6) create docs/guides/ templates, (7) update README.md
Brian says: use docs/guides/ not docs/examples/ — feels more user-facing
Brian says: implement bdc export now — it fixes the broken hooks
Should we implement bdc export as part of this work, or keep scope to docs only?
BDC_GUIDE.md still references cc:opus-4.5 everywhere, only shows Linear refs (no jira/gh), and lacks cross-session resumption workflow
The git hooks in init.go reference 'bdc export --quiet' but no such command exists — cmd/bdc/export.go was never created, and ListAllDependencies() is missing from the store
monterrey-v1 has battle-tested bdc integration (docs, hooks, .gitignore) that the open source repo is missing — porting these could make bdc work out of the box for any project
